section.heading: Saving provision
section.chapeau: The provisions of this Part 11 See References in Text note below. shall not be construed to—

paragraph.content: limit or otherwise affect any authority for the making of any appointment to, or for fixing or adjusting the pay for, any position for which the pay is disbursed by the Chief Administrative Officer of the House of Representatives; or
affect the continuity of employment of, or reduce the pay of, any employee whose pay is disbursed by the Chief Administrative Officer of the House.

note.heading: References in Text
note.p: This Part, referred to in text, means Part 7 (§§ 471–477) of title IV of Pub. L. 91–510, Oct. 26, 1970, 84 Stat. 1193. For complete classification of Part 7 to the Code, see Tables.

note.heading: Codification
note.p: Section was formerly classified to section 336 of this title prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 1996—Pars. (1), (2). Pub. L. 104–186 substituted “Chief Administrative Officer” for “Clerk”.

note.heading: Effective Date
note.p: Section effective immediately prior to noon on Jan. 3, 1971, see section 601(1) of Pub. L. 91–510, set out as an Effective Date of 1970 Amendment note under section 4301 of this title.
